senses.definition: used to imply that one should focus on taking care of their own family and community first, before extending acts of kindness to others

word_list.word: a gentle heart is tied with an easy thread
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ dʒˈɛntəl hˈɑːɹt ɪz tˈaɪd wɪð ɐn ˈiːzi θɹˈɛd

senses.definition: used to imply that kind and compassionate people can form effortless and meaningful connections with others, as they are more receptive to simple acts of kindness and affection

word_list.word: do unto others as you would have them do unto you
word_list.pronunciation: dˈuː ˌʌntʊ ˈʌðɚz æz juː wʊdhɐv ðˌɛm dˈuː ˌʌntʊ juː

senses.definition: used to emphasize that one should treat others in the same way that one would like to be treated themselves
used to imply that there is a strong connection between the emotions of pity and love, as feeling sorry for someone can lead to empathy and affection toward them

word_list.word: love covers a multitude of sins
word_list.pronunciation: lˈʌv kˈʌvɚz ɐ mˈʌltɪtˌuːd ʌv sˈɪnz

senses.definition: used to imply that when one truly loves another, they are more likely to overlook or forgive their faults or mistakes, emphasizing the power of love to overcome shortcomings or flaws

word_list.word: to he that is warm thinks all so
word_list.pronunciation: hiː ðæt ɪz wˈɔːɹm θˈɪŋks ˈɔːl sˈoʊ

senses.definition: used to suggest that when someone is comfortable or satisfied, they may view things more positively and underestimate the difficulties faced by others

word_list.word: it takes one to know one
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt tˈeɪks wˈʌn tə nˈoʊ wˌʌn

senses.definition: used to suggest that in order to truly understand someone, one must possess similar qualities, experiences, or characteristics themselves

word_list.word: to know all is to forgive all
word_list.pronunciation: tə nˈoʊ ˈɔːl ɪz tə fəɡˈɪv ˈɔːl

senses.definition: used to imply that if one understands the full context or circumstances behind someone's actions or behavior, they will be more likely to forgive them

word_list.word: life is too short to make others' shorter
word_list.pronunciation: lˈaɪf ɪz tˈuː ʃˈɔːɹt tə mˌeɪk ˈʌðɚz ʃˈɔːɹɾɚ

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of treating others with kindness and respect, and avoiding actions that cause harm or suffering, recognizing the finite and precious nature of life

word_list.word: there but for the grace of God go I
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ bˌʌt fɚðə ɡɹˈeɪs ʌv ɡˈɑːd ɡˌoʊ ˈaɪ

senses.definition: used to encourage gratitude and empathy, reminding individuals that they could have been in a similar situation as those facing difficulties if not for external factors beyond their control

examples.x: As he heard of a friend's misfortune, he remembered the saying 'there but for the grace of God go I,' acknowledging that he too could have faced similar difficulties if not for the blessings in his life.
